title: Part 1
text: [02] Timeline & DOOM Clock // These events are what happens if the party does not act. TIMELINE 1850 -The region’s largest mine opens in Carpenter, OR. 1893 -A rash of deaths in the mine makes it unprosperous. 1933 - Carpenter is hit hard by drought and The Great Depression. Continued disappearances feed into local legends. 1937 - Carpenter is chosen as one of the sites of The New Deal, bringing jobs and reinvestment to the area. 1960 -The mall is built atop the old mine. It becomes a thriving pillar of the community. 1987 -The mall experiences its first big-box closure. 1989 -Mr. Neil(p. 6) is hired to manage the mall. DOOM CLOCK DOOM Clock (1-10): A measure on how close the party is to DOOM. Actions taken by the PCs change the progression (and details) of the DOOM clock. DOOM 1 -It’s 7pm! Visitors gather at Contest Stage (p. 12) for the raffle and speeches. DOOM 2 - The mall fully phases out of reality. A Child of Ammon (p. 9) emerges, killing at least one NPC. DOOM 3 -People quickly realize a Barrier (p. 12) has trapped them in the mall. DOOM 4 -Factions (p. 6) emerge and begin to amass resources. DOOM 5 -A Child of Ammon (p. 9) is caught mid-gestation, inside of a victim. DOOM 6 -Power goes out. Water supply becomes finite. DOOM 7 - The REPLACED (p. 8) begin sabotaging the mall’s supplies. The fleshy mass- of-pets will escape the pet shop. DOOM 8 -Water is sabotaged and ceases to flow from plumbing. DOOM 9 - Fungal Fields (p. 26) appear throughout the mall, sprouting from spores and the dead. DOOM 10 - With enough bodies, the Portal (p. 27) becomes a breach and the mall phases back to reality. SUMMER 1999 1999 - Last month, Mr. Neil(p. 6) began kidnapping and sacrificing people. He used some to build a portal in the minesbelow the mall’s Bunker (p. 23). He turned the rest into Fruiting Bodies (p. 13). They’re in the vents now, shedding spores to infect others. 1999 - Last week, Mr. Neil brought forth a Child of Ammon (p. 9), a piece of a creature from beyond the portal. It is gestating inside of him. 1999 - Today, the community is attempting to save the failing mall by raffling off an Oldsmobile. This module starts at DOOM 1. Timeline Credits & Table of Contents // Those who helped create this modern horror dungeon. [03] THE MALL - REMASTERED Writing, Layout & Design: Goblin Archives © 2024 Editing: Vi Huntsman Proofreading: Jarrett Crader Cover: Zach Hazard Vaupen © 2022 Illustrations: Garbage Goat (p. 11, 12, 13, 15, 17, 18, 20) © 2022 Illustration: Trevor Henderson (p. 10) © 2022 Additional Cartography/Graphic: Luke Saunders (p. 21-27) © 2024 Playtesters: Chris, Will, Geoff, Andy, Paul, Tyler, Nick, Shadow and John Consulting: Chrissy Crits, Will Conwell, Garbage Goat, Logan Dean, Josh Domanski. TABLE OF CONTENTS Timeline02 DOOM CLOCK02 Visitor Map04 Store Directory: Shops & Personnel05 Factions & NPCs06 Dying in the Mall08 Have They Been REPLACED?08 Children of Ammon09 Locations11 Level 112 Level 216 Level 319 Basement and Lower Tunnels Map21 The Basement22 The Bunker and What Lay Below23 Fungal Fields26 The Portal27 Encounters28 Custom Fallout30 Nightmares 31 Character Creation32 Ways To Change This Module35 Whisper Cards37 Quick Reference39 [04] Visitors Map // Based on the Lloyd Center in Portland, OR. You can use your own mall as the setting too. MAIN ENTRANCE DMV POLLY’S PET EMPORIUM BUSINESS ASSOCIATION EMMY’S SWORDS SKATE WORLD MAINTENANCE LOADING DOCKS FREIGHT ELEVATOR YOUR BODY IS A TEMPLE WELESS CLINIC SECURITY FOOD COURT HELP DESK STAN’S PRETZELS MANAGER’S OFFICE JOE’S BURGER SPIRAL CINEMA BREAK ROOM MOOTSY’S ARCADE STAGE PARKING GARAGE 1 PARKING GARAGE 1 PARKING GARAGE 2 PARKING GARAGE 2 PARKING GARAGE 3 PARKING GARAGE 3 PARKING GARAGE 3 LEVEL 1 LEVEL 2 LEVEL 3 PARKING GARAGE 1 THE MALL: VISITORS MAP Directory & Reference // Players create additional stores and NPCs at character creation. [05] STORE DIRECTORY PERSONNEL 1. Mr. Neil - Mall Manager (p. 6) 2. Billy - Reports directly to Mr. Neil (p. 6) 3. Kurt - Stan’s Pretzel Stand (p. 8) 4. Maggie - Head of the CBB (p. 6) 5. Griffith -Your Body is a Temple (p. 7) 6. Samuel - Griffith’s right hand (p. 7) 7. William- Head mall walker (p. 6) 8. Bruce - Maintenance (p. 8) 9. 10. 11. Beth - Head of the Mall Rats (p. 7) 12. 13. 14. Ruth - Patron (p. 7) Emmy - Emmy’s Swords & Stuff (p. 8) Logan - DMV Express (p. 8) RULES & REFERENCE Liminal Horror Rules: Annotations for core rules are noted as (LH p. xx). PDF is hyperlinked. Character Creation(p. 32): Custom Mall- specific Character Creation procedures. Custom Fallouts(p. 30): Mall-specific Fallouts, to be used in place of, or in addition to, the core Fallouts. Whispers Cards(p. 37): Secret prompts triggered by character action (including every Fallout and often replacing Stress). A few cards cause a PC to become REPLACED. Nightmare & Prophecies(p. 31): To sleep is to dream. All PCs make CTRL saves when sleeping. Failing means rolling on the Nightmare Table. Have They Been REPLACED?(p. 8): NPCs may be REPLACED, roll a d10 against the DOOM level. If the result is equal or under AND they fail a CTRL save, they have been REPLACED. As the adventure progresses, NPCs’ CTRL may decrease. Mall Size: 1,472,000 sq ft, 178 mostly-vacant shop fronts, 3 floors, domed skylight, 3 garages. Number of NPCs: Approximately 100 visitors and business owners. Utilities: Electricity via generators (backup fuel in the basement). Water via cistern and pump. Vents: Ventilation shafts web through the mall, connecting rooms and levels. Level 1 1. Contest Stage (p. 12) 2. Outside the Mall (p. 12) 3. Parking Garages - all levels (p. 13) 4. Vents - all levels (p. 13) 5. Maintenance (p. 14) 6. Loading Docks (p. 14) 7. Your Body is a Temple (p. 14) 8. Polly’s Pet Emporium (p. 15) 9. DMV Express (p. 15) 10. Skate World (p. 15) 11. Mootsy’s Arcade (p. 15) Level 2 12. Break Room (p. 16) 13. Security (p. 17) 14. Business Association (p. 18) 15. Emmy’s Swords & Stuff (p. 18) Level 3 16. The Manager’s Office (p. 20) 17. Food Court (p. 21) 18. Stan’s Pretzel Stand (p. 21) 19. Joe’s Burger Barn (p. 21) 20. Spiral Cinema (p. 21) Basement 22. Utility Room (p. 22) 23. Mall Rats Clubhouse (p. 22) 24. Bunker & Tunnels (p. 23) FACILITATING Become familiar with the mall’s layout, the NPCs(p. 6) and the DOOM clock(p. 2). Use the Character Creation (p. 32) steps to create PCs, get a rumor, introduce a custom NPC and create a shop for the mall. At the start of play it’s 7pm. The DOOM clock starts ticking and the crowd gathers at #1 The Contest Stage. Terry - Night Security (p. 8) Hudson - Head of Security (p. 7) FACTIONS & NPCS After the initial events (DOOM 1 - 3), factions quickly form. Each faction has broad goals and actions, while its individual members have varying responses to DOOM 3+. Most NPCs are listed under their chosen faction, while others are listed in Unaffiliated NPCs. To usher the Children of Ammon (p. 9) into our world, so they can prepare the final sacrifice. ▶ Actions: They kidnapped people to turn into Fruiting Bodies (p. 13) and build the portal below the mall. ▶ Base:Manager’s Office (p. 20) and the Tunnels (p. 23). ▶Mr. Neil (he/him) - Mall Manager STR: 14 DEX: 10 CTRL: 14 HP: 5 Armor: 1 Revolver (d6) �Middle aged, lean, constantly fidgeting, off putting. Wears ill fitting tan suits (muddy hems). �He has been REPLACED (p. 8). �Carries the Manager’s Office key and a vague letter apologizing to an unnamed recipient. �Crisis Response: Does everything in his power to convert more into Children of Ammon (p. 9). �Location:Manager’s Office (p. 19) or the Tunnels (p. 23). ▶ Billy (he/they) - Mr. Neil’s Assistant Switchblade (d6) �Dirty coveralls, clipboard filled with tasks, hair covering face. �Aided Mr. Neil in the kidnappings and sacrifices. �Crisis Response: Helps convert others. �Location: the Loading Docks (p. 14) or Access Hallways (p. 11). To protect their capital above all else. To assert control by maintaining old hierarchies. ▶ Actions: Will quickly resort to violence to assert order. They will fortify their office and destroy any nearby cameras before attempting to take the security office. ▶ Base:Business Association(p. 18). ▶ Maggie (she/her) - Coalition of Business Owners STR: 12 DEX: 14 CTRL: 13 HP: 4 Armor: 1 Revolver (d6) �Sharp suit, Gucci glasses, heels. �Was quietly organizing a fiscal takeover of the mall pre-disaster. �Crisis Response: Might makes right. Uses her pre-crisis position as leverage to lead during this crisis. Weakness will be squashed quickly and publicly. Will not hesitate to enact violence. �Locations: Fortifying the Business Association(p. 18). Gathering people who are of use. Taking over Security (p. 17). ▶ William (he/him) - Mall Walker �Older, Floral shirt, short shorts. �Retired young and has enjoyed every minute of it. �Crisis Response: Becomes extremely accusatory of others. �Location: Around the mall. ▶ Terry (he/him) - Night Security STR: 8 DEX: 8 CTRL: 8 HP: 3 Armor: 1 Taser (d6, nonlethal) �Middle aged, scowling. �Amassed contraband for himself pre-disaster. �Crisis Response: Vies for extreme measures. �Location: Breaking into stores. DOOM 5 leads charge in taking of Security (p. 17). AMMON’S CHOSEN COALITION OF BUSINESS OWNERS [06] Factions & NPCS // Maggie will lead her faction on a brutal campaign for control: Might = Right. Factions & NPCs // Mall Rats = save and survive; Griffith will use fear and superstition to amass power. [07] Movement & Wellness Clinic. Members wear matching pastel-purple tracksuits and white Nikes. Quickly shifts from wellness & movement focus to being an End of Days cult convinced of salvation through rebirth. ▶ Actions: Quickly collect resources (for the “greater good”). ▶ Base: Your Body Is a Temple (p. 14). ▶ Griffith (he/they) - Head Instructor �Warm smile and striking gray eyes. Sharp features. �His gray tracksuit has silver piping. �Exudes calm. Charismatic. �Crisis Response: It was a sign. Leverages devoted followers. Gives outsiders one chance to join the path before treating them as outcasts. �Locations: Coordinating his followers from within Your Body Is a Temple (p. 14). ▶ Samuel (he/him) - Evening Instructor �Stout, tightly cropped buzz cut. �Believes in Griffith’s vision, wants to make it a reality. �Crisis Response: Uses this as an excuse to mete out violence, manifesting Griffith’s desires with any means necessary. �Location: Leading teams to gather supplies. ▶ Hudson (he/him) - Head of Security STR: 11 DEX: 11 CTRL: 8 HP: 3 Armor: 1 Taser (d6, nonlethal) �Young, flustered. �Takes pride in his job, but isn’t very good at it. �Crisis Response: Freezes, unable to take action. Does not rise to the occasion. �Location: Crying in Security (p. 17), trying to find where in the manual it covers a situation like this. Without help, seeks out Griffith to lead him. YOUR BODY IS A TEMPLE To survive. To stick it to the business owners. Will attempt to avoid any other major faction and wait out the problem. Use the vent system to get around the mall (pre/post crisis). ▶ Actions: Gather supplies, build a group of “stable” survivors. ▶ Base:Clubhouse(p. 22). ▶ Beth (she/her) - Mall Rat STR: 10 DEX: 14 CTRL: 12 HP: 4 Armor: 1 Brass knuckles (d6) �Trans, flannel, t-shirt, baggy jeans, skateboard. �Constantly butts heads with management but is liked by employees. �Quick and rarely caught off guard.. �Crisis Response: Encourages others to avoid threats, stick together and get items necessary for survival. Helps others when push comes to shove. �Locations: Vents (p. 13). Seeking aid from the PCs to rescue her friend from the vent. Clubhouse(p. 22). ▶ Ruth (she/her) - Mall Patron �Golden Girls. �Used to work at the mall, knows all the goings on. �Crisis Response: Uses logic and calm to survive. Wants to make sure the Mall Rats are safe, then figure out what’s going on. �Location: Around the mall. MALL RATS ▶ Bruce (he/him) - Maintenance �Young. Coveralls and tool-belt. �Easy to talk to. �Did not like Billy (p. 6). �Crisis Response: Survive at all costs, uses knowledge of mall. �Location: Maintenance (p. 14). ▶ Emmy (she/her) - Armorer �Trans, spiked chainmail over flannel, hair in a bun. �Crisis Response: To protect her shop (her magnum opus). �Location: Bartering weapons for resources from Emmy’s Swords & Stuff (p. 18). ▶ Kurt (he/him) - Assistant Manager �Older, soft spoken, long hair. �Pretzel stand’s last employee. �Rumors are true: Kurt has in fact, “seen some shit.” �Crisis Response: Takes decisive action. Thinks the factions are as dangerous as the monster. �Location: Searching for supplies. Fortifying Stan’s Pretzel Stand (p. 20). ▶ Logan (he/him) - DMV Employee �White button down, black tie and pants, thick glasses. �Knows every conspiracy theory. �Believes that the mysterious Bureau is involved in some way in what has been going on. �Crisis Response: “You can’t trust anyone if it can look like anything!” �Location:DMV (p. 15). Out seeking those who desire truth. Whether benefiting from the chaos, trying to solve the problem, accusing others or just trying to survive, these NPCs are not aligned with any faction. UNAFFILIATED NPCS DYING IN THE MALL Taking Critical Damage or experiencing extreme pain in the mall causes PCs and NPCs to pierce the veil. As the portal and its spores lose hold, the person briefly “resyncs” and glimpses the real world. While risky and hard to replicate, maintaining extreme levels of damage will allow a PC to break through the Barrier (p. 12), in exchange for permanent bodily damage. REPLACED To determine if an NPC has been REPLACED: ▶Roll a d10 against the DOOM level and make a CTRL save. �If the result is equal or under AND they fail a CTRL save, they have been REPLACED. As the adventure progresses, NPCs’ CTRL may decrease. QUICK REFERENCE Population: Approximately 100 employees and customers trapped in the mall at the start of this module. Baseline Stats: Unless otherwise noted, Ability Scores are 10 (STR, DEX, CTRL) and 3 HP. For advice on modifying stats, see Investigators: Creating Monsters. Weapons: Unarmed strikes deal d4, and most weapons deal d6. For more, see Investigators: Equipment List. [08] Unaffiliated NPCS & Mechanics // Pain unclouds one’s vision. CHILDREN OF AMMON // Wet, gooey, and gross. Frame their appearances like the PCs are in a film. [09] MONSTER TABLE - 3d8 Use this table to inspire the monsters’ different iterations. It has no standard anatomy and can take on many forms before replacing the people around it. MULTIPLE TWISTED FACES ERUPTING SPINE FUNGAL STALKS TUMOROUS GROWTHS WINGS OF BONE SKINLESS DISTENDING BISECTED PULSING JAGGED OPENINGS IMPOSSIBLE ANGLES WHIPPING TUBES MULTI-HINGED JAW FLESH SPIDER CLAWS SPURS OF BONE APPENDAGES EMERGING MELTING UNFOLDING PREHENSILE LIMBS SPEWING SPORES BLOOMING CRAB-LIKE WRITHING MASS To bring all flesh into itself. To throw open the portal beneath the mall. CHILDREN OF AMMON DRIVE When consumed and replicated, the victim becomes REPLACED. By all outward appearance they seem perfectly normal, but act now as a Child of Ammon. THE REPLACED The only sure way to know if someone has been REPLACED is to kill them. Near bodily death, a Child of Ammon will attempt to save a piece of itself. THE FLESH IS WEAK Destroying the portal will neutralize the spores within the mall’s denizens, but it will not eradicate The Children of Ammon that are already on the loose. IF EVEN A CELL GET’S OUT! It prefers to integrate and assimilate out of sight, dragging fresh or living victims away (often into the vents). THIS FLESH WAS ONCE YOURS ▶HP: 3-5 (flesh quantity/raw size) ▶Armor: +1-2 (bone shards/fungus) ▶STR: 12+ (size) ▶DEX: 10+ (size and number of limbs) ▶CTRL: 13 ▶Damage: d6 - d12 (number of appendages, size, bones, fungus). Metamorphosis: Can change forms in moments, sometimes even breaking off into multiple unique instances. Anointed Blood: Spray a mix of blood, and spores. Target takes a Whisper Card (p. 37), witnesses take d4 Stress. Critical Damage: Fleshy tendrils pierce the victim’s flesh, worming their way between muscle and bone (D4 damage to DEX). STATS Locations // The mall is massive. It is a necropolis to failing capitalism. [11] LOCATIONS GENERAL FEATURES ▶Stores have glass facades. ▶Storefront security gates that can be lowered and locked. ▶Store Keys: �At least one store employee has a key. �Maintenance (p. 14) has a copy of each individual key. �Security (p. 17) is supposed to have a copy of each key. �Manager’s Office (p. 19) has a master key. (false rumor) ▶Most store have rear doors that connect to the access hallways. ▶ Each store has speakers connected to Security (p. 17). ▶ Vents, Access Hallways and Parking Garages connect to all 3 floors. ACCESS HALLWAYS (ALL LEVELS) ▶Not fully included on the map, they spider-web throughout the mall and connect to most stores. ▶Primary access to Loading Docks (p. 14), Break Room (p. 16) and between levels. ▶A maintenance stairwell connects all floors to the Basement (p. 21). ▶Access to long-abandoned rooms now used as storage. �One stores oxygen tanks from a failed “spa” that took investor money and ran. �Others contain supplies from closed shops (at first). A hastily set up stage and a “Save The Mall” banner, adjacent to Skate World Roller Rink(p. 15). ▶A 1973 Oldsmobile Delta 88, parked at the stage’s base. DOOM 1-2 ▶7pm: Visitors gather for the raffle Mr. Neil(p. 6) gives a speech. ▶It is cut short by flashing of orange lights followed by an loud crash. ▶A Child of Ammon erupts from behind the stage, attacking and killing an NPC. One option is to have a NPC that is connected to the players (p. 34). ▶The crowd devolves into chaos as Maggie(p. 6) tries to coordinate an escape. 1. CONTEST STAGE An orange writhing mass of bubbling flesh, encompassing the mall. �First time it is witnessed (1 Stress). �Touching the barrier triggers a Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶A shadow moves on the other side. ▶Its sickly light is always visible from the glass doors and skylight. ▶Damaging it only reveals more of the barrier before it heals itself, consuming anything within reach. ▶Two people are slowly being consumed by the barrier. �Extreme levels of pain creates opportunity to break through. �See Dying in the Mall(p. 8). 2. BARRIER MAIN ENTRANCE DMV POLLY’S PET EMPORIUM SKATE WORLD MAINTENANCE LOADING DOCKS YOUR BODY IS A TEMPLE WELLNESS CLINIC MOOTSY’S ARCADE STAGE PARKING GARAGE 1 PARKING GARAGE 2 PARKING GARAGE 3 LEVEL 1 [12] Level 1 // This is where it all begins. The barrier, the first Child, your PCs’ first WHISPER CARD. FREIGHT ELEVATOR Fruiting Bodies are the answer to where the missing people have gone. Embrace the horror. [13] The mall’s massive circulatory system. ▶Grilles (36” x 40”, half the size of a door) litter the mall. They are hinged, with most security screws missing. ▶Most vents are comfortably crawlable, with "ladders" granting access to upper levels. ▶Some vents are narrow, impeding easy movement, others have sudden drops which run risk of falling (d4 damage). FRUITING BODIES bloomfrom missing townspeople in the vents. ▶Encountering a Fruiting Body triggers a Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶A faint haze of fungal spores fills the air around the bodies. ▶The emaciated bodies are spatchcocked but still “alive.” �Fleshy-fungal growths explode out of chest and throat. �Strands of viscera and fungus web out, keeping them affixed to the vent. �Each “breath”releases a cloud of bright yellow spores that get sucked down into the vent. ▶Odd symbols, smeared with blood, are carved into the metal around each body. �They match those in the manager's notebook (p. 19) and Billy’s “room” (p. 14) - (Clue). ▶Patches of mud trail away from the bodies - (Clue). Deeper into the vents smell distinctly of earth and minerals. ▶They lead deeper and deeper, eventually to the Ventilation Access (p. 24) in the mines. �Entering this lower level triggers a Whisper Card (p. 37). 4. THE VENTS (ALL LEVELS) Three massive multi-story garages, built to confuse. ▶20 cars remain within. ▶Near an exit, a car crashed into the Barrier (p. 12) and has now been mostly consumed. ▶A sprinter van, its engine running, is backed up against a Vent. �Inside is a Fruiting Body and a makeshift air distribution system - Whisper Card (p. 37). 3. PARKING GARAGES d10Search Car Table 01Cassettes 02Pistol 03Duct Tape 04First Aid Kit 05Tactical Flashlight 06Can of Gas 07Bolt Cutters 08 Chainsaw, Can of Gas, Extendable Tree Pruning Pole 09Bags of Groceries 10Ornate Amulet Each car has a car battery, jumper cables, and tire iron (d6). Medium-sized workshop area. Buzzing fluorescent lights swing gently in sputtering air from the vent. ▶Crammed full of mall storage, leaving only a small corner for the workshop. ▶Tools and raw materials meticulously organized (50% chance to find any tool). �One chainsaw (bulky) with 2 cans of gasoline. �A welding torch, safety equipment (+1 armor), 2x fuel. ▶A combination lockbox hanging by the door contains a copy of each store key. ▶A well stocked first aid kit hangs on the wall. ▶A board fills half the wall with safety signage, and a to-do list - (Clue). �Clear lower store room. �Find the cause of all the mud. �Add screws to vent grilles. �Find source of smell. 5. MAINTENANCE Where trucks are off-loaded and their goods distributed throughout the mall. ▶Includes a staging area, storage, Billy’s office, freight elevator and access hallways. 6A. Billy’s Office located off of the loading dock. ▶Nudie posters cover the walls. ▶Scrawled on top of the posters, covering every inch of the space, are geometric symbols - (Clue). �Upon entry (1 Stress). ▶Investigating symbols trigger a Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶A small cot and TV fill most of the space. Under the cot is: �Billy's ID tag. �Sawed-off Shotgun (d6 blast, bulky). �Heavy-duty key on a leather necklace, specked with mud- (Clue). �Tackle box with 6 shotgun shells. 6B. Freight Elevator located off of the loading dock. ▶Connects the Access Hallways (p. 11) to each floor, including the Basement (p. 21). ▶Florescent light flickers incessantly. ▶Specks of blood, mud, and mold stain the floor of the elevator. ▶Some bloody fingernails are embedded in the tattered padding along the walls. 6. LOADING DOCKS The outer facade is filled with screens playing instructional jazzercise intercut with motivational mantras and smooth jazz. ▶Large open room, shelves lined with supplements, vitamins, shoes and pastel purple tracksuits. ▶Large stage with three screens hanging behind it. ▶Door near the stage leads to a back. ▶The back rooms have quiet smooth jazz music dubbed with upbeat “guided meditation.” The space is divided into: �A bunk room for the members. �A rec room playing recorded “workouts” on loop. �Two single bathrooms, retrofitted with shower heads. �A small room that acts as Griffith’s sleeping quarters and office. �A closet that has been converted into a video recording studio. 6. YOUR BODY IS A TEMPLE: MOVEMENT AND WELLNESS CLINIC [14] Level 1 // Maintenance and the Loading Dock have a lot of clues and resources for players. Open floor plan, free-range petting zoo style petshop (but dangerous animals are locked up). It is a mix of standard and extremely esoteric animals. ▶The shop is dark. Its flimsy lock will permanently break if forced open. ▶Audible sound of animals crying to get out. ▶Aisles filled to the brim with aquarium and pet cages, many of which are open. ▶The floor is covered in highly flammable pee-soaked sawdust. ▶Audible sound of animals crying to get out. �In the dark, a roiling chimera of fused-together animals. �It will attack any who venture within. �STR: 15 DEX 7 HP: 6 Bite (D6, blast) 8. POLLY’S PET EMPORIUM Level 1 // Polly’s Pets is some good 80s animal based body horror. Seed something going on inside. [15] Faded purple carpet, neon lighting and arcade cabinets fill this large room. ▶Toward the back, a small stage, light and sound system for concerts. ▶A tiny back storage room has been painted green to double as a green room. �Music equipment is stored here. �Multiple large CO2 tanks are stored near an industrial fog machine. 11. MOOTSY’S ARCADE & MUSIC Bright buzzing fluorescent lights, small chairs, a red take-a-number and a help desk. ▶Every inch of this space is wired with cameras and audio equipment (live feed to the break room computer). ▶A small “research” station is set up in the storage closet. ▶One wall is dominated by a corkboard of local legends and myths. �The word BELOW THE MALL circled, witharrows pointing to many different subterranean based legends -Whisper Card (p. 37). �Pictures of Federal Agents in and around Carpenter. “Is THE BUREAU involved?” is underlined heavily. 9. DMV EXPRESS The mall’s central attraction. ▶A DJ booth is next to the enter/exit panels. ▶Benches are affixed in front of a small service window. �Through the opening, racks and racks of rentable roller skates are visible. 10. SKATE WORLD ROLLER RINK Rows of poorly-lit employee lockers, a couple old couches and scattered folding tables. ▶The flimsy combination locks can be broken with tools or leverage. ▶If searching random lockers, roll (or choose) 3 pieces of Starting Gear (p. 33). Billy's Locker: Flecks of mud on the exterior - (Clue). ▶Small planner lists an appointment from two days ago with Mr. Neil (p. 6) circled in red. ▶Muddy boots and gloves (examination reveals flecks of blood and orange fuzz) - Whisper Card (p. 37). Emmy’s Locker: STAY OUT written in striking letters. The lock is stronger than standard. ▶The door has an unusual resistance and an audible click, if opened. It is rigged with a crossbow trap (d6 damage). ▶“WARNED YOU” written in neon spray paint. Logan’s Locker: A weird rune is carved on this locker. ▶The inside is an evidence board filled with sketches, articles, printouts and a mass of red string. ▶Two modified walkie talkies hang from the coat hooks. Bruce’s Locker: Noticeably shinier and cleaner than the rest. ▶A notebook of observations about the mall. ▶One section details Mr. Neil’s odd behavior. ▶Another notes the areas where mud has been tracked: � Basement entrance. �3 vent openings (near vents that have been acting up). �The loading dock. ▶His notes imply that Mr. Neilis the main culprit - (Clue). 12. BREAK ROOM LEVEL 2 [16] Level 2 // Employee lockers: It’s like shopping in other people’s stuff. BUSINESS ASSOCIATION EMMY’S SWORDS SECURITY BREAK ROOM Level 2 // The cameras in Security are a great way to build tension and give information. [17] MONITORING ROOM ▶Large dark room filled with old, rewired screens. ▶ Hudson (p. 7) is holed up here but avoids looking at the screens. ▶ Doom Clock % (p. 2) chance any given feed has gone dark. ▶ Watching the Feeds: �Time watching reveals two Childrenof Ammon(p. 9) on different screensat the same time. �A Child of Ammon (p. 9) mutilating a NPC before dragging them offscreen - Whisper Card (p. 37). �Later, the NPC is adamant nothing is wrong. Only in “death” will the monster reveal itself. ▶An older microphone and keypad broadcasts over the speakers throughout the mall and shops. Computer terminal keeps backups and can play multiple feeds concurrently on the screens. ▶ If searching for specific information, the only risk is time spent looking for info that could be caught on film. ▶ If searching randomly, roll on table: An early target of most factions. Multiple parties will be vying for resources here by DOOM 5 (p. 2). ▶Divided into three rooms. SMALL FRONT OFFICE ▶Two desks stacked high with paperwork. ▶Security lockers. �4 batons (d6). �A flak vest (+1 Armor). �4 handcuffs. ▶A bright red, mostly-full first aid kit hangs on the wall. ▶Lockbox filled with contraband. ▶ Hudson’s locker: �The inside is wallpapered with a vision board, a smiling Hudson at its center. ▶ Terry’s locker: �2 broken skateboards. �CCTV printout of three teens skating, two of the figures crossed off in red. BATHROOM ▶A retrofitted shower toilet stall. ▶Hidden inside the toilet tank are 4 syringes of a mystery substance. �A homemade drug cocktail.
